pet.name: Mochi
pet.special_needs:
Mild arthritis in the back legs. Needs joint-friendly exercise, daily
    mobility observation, monthly flea/tick prevention, and weekly weight
    monitoring. Occasionally has reduced appetite after very active days.
pet.characteristics: Golden Retrievers are highly social, energetic dogs requiring daily moderate-to-vigorous exercise and mental enrichment through play, training, and retrieval activities. They thrive on consistent routines, respond well to positive reinforcement, and benefit from regular socialization. Diet should be portion-controlled and balanced; feeding twice daily is typical. They shed heavily year-round and require weekly brushing and regular grooming. Routine veterinary care, monthly parasite prevention, and early joint-health monitoring support longevity. They are sensitive to heat and prefer cooler environments. Regular weight management and low-impact exercise routines help prevent or slow joint issues common in the breed.
pet.species_profile_summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in good health; they are active, social dogs requiring consistent exercise, grooming, and preventive veterinary care.

age_context.summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in good health; they are active, social dogs requiring consistent exercise, grooming, and preventive veterinary care.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning and evening feeding
raw_recommendations.notes: Twice-daily portion-controlled meals; monitor appetite especially after active days.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Golden Retrievers require consistent twice-daily feeding; senior pets benefit from routine and appetite monitoring.

raw_recommendations.name: Mobility and gait observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Watch for limping, stiffness, or difficulty rising; note any changes in movement or comfort.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Mild arthritis requires daily observation to track progression and catch changes early.

raw_recommendations.name: Joint-friendly exercise and play
raw_recommendations.notes: Low-impact activity like gentle walks or swimming; avoid high-impact jumping; adjust intensity based on daily mobility.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Golden Retrievers need daily moderate exercise; joint-friendly routines support arthritis management and prevent stiffness.

raw_recommendations.name: Energy and appetite check-in
raw_recommendations.notes: Record energy level, water intake, and any appetite changes; note if reduced after active days.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Observation tasks create a record for senior pets and help detect early signs of decline.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly weight check
raw_recommendations.notes: Weigh Mochi at the same time each week; track trends to support joint health and prevent obesity.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management is critical for Golden Retrievers with arthritis; weekly monitoring supports early intervention.

raw_recommendations.name: Brushing and coat care
raw_recommendations.notes: Golden Retrievers shed heavily; weekly brushing reduces shedding and allows skin inspection.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Breed-standard grooming supports skin health and provides opportunity to check for lumps or irritation.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly flea and tick prevention
raw_recommendations.notes: Apply or administer monthly parasite prevention as prescribed; mark calendar for consistent scheduling.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Routine parasite prevention is essential preventive care for senior dogs.

raw_recommendations.name: Bathroom habits and hydration observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Track bathroom frequency and water intake; report major changes to veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from consistent monitoring of bathroom and hydration habits to catch health changes early.

blocked_recommendations.name: Monthly flea and tick prevention
reasons: Recommendation contains unsafe or unsupported medical advice.
